qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable Frogger with traffic, river hazards, homes, timer, lives, scoring, and restart flow, but movement interpolation is flawed and some visuals/layout details are only approximate rather than arcade-authentic.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An excellent implementation featuring smooth grid-based movement, diving turtles, and a functional timer-based scoring system.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core mechanics are present but the frog movement interpolation has a bug where frog.x/y are mutated during lerp instead of using separate display coordinates, causing position drift; river support detection and collision logic have edge cases that can cause unexpected deaths; timer bar and lives overlap visually; home bay landing works but level progression feels rough.

qa_scores_gpt54.notes: Playable Frogger with working movement, hazards, homes, timer, lives, pause and restart, but visuals are only approximate and there are a few logic/polish issues such as duplicate death sounds, odd lane/layout mapping, and some inconsistent object setup.

qa_scores_gemini.notes: An exceptionally well-implemented Frogger clone with accurate grid-snapping, platform carrying logic, diving turtles, and a polished audio-visual experience.

qa_scores_opus.notes: Core Frogger mechanics are implemented with grid-snapped movement, vehicle/log/turtle lanes, home bays, timer, lives, and level progression, but collision detection relies on exact y-value matching which can be fragile, the hop lock timing creates occasional unresponsiveness, and the river carry/drowning checks may misfire due to floating-point row comparisons.
